roadmap_text: Files Touched: crates/hippo-daemon/src/daemon.rs, claude_session.rs, native_messaging.rs
objection: native_messaging.rs does not belong in P0.2's file list. 01-source-health.md §Write Paths explicitly states: 'native_messaging.rs does NOT write directly to SQLite... The source_health update for browser therefore occurs in flush_events, not in native_messaging.rs.' 08-anti-patterns.md reinforces this.
evidence: grep -n 'rusqlite|Connection' crates/hippo-daemon/src/native_messaging.rs returns 0 hits. The actual merged P0.2 (PR #68, commit 67dbe16) touched exactly 6 files: storage.rs, claude_session.rs, daemon.rs, lib.rs, and two test files — native_messaging.rs absent.
suggested_fix: Drop native_messaging.rs from the Files Touched cell in 07-roadmap.md. List only daemon.rs and claude_session.rs to match what actually shipped.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED — merged PR confirms the objection. 07-roadmap.md still has the stale file list; fix with a follow-up docs-only edit.

roadmap_text: P0.2 Effort: ~4h
objection: 4h for P0.2 is 3–5× too low. A single developer cannot ship this scope in 4h given the review-cycle revisions required.
evidence: PR #68 landed via 10 commits (1 initial + 9 fix commits). The fix commits addressed: Ok(-1) duplicate insertion, idle-tick heartbeat-vs-success semantics, recompute_rolling_counts shutdown + first-tick, last_heartbeat_ts scope (browser-only), partial-success tick gating, pre-migration error differentiation, test-comment accuracy, heartbeat column semantics.
suggested_fix: Revise the historical estimate to ~1–1.5 days. For future similar work, budget explicit review-cycle hours rather than a raw single-pass estimate.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED — real lifecycle was multi-day with 6 review-driven fix commits. Keep as a historical calibration data point for P1/P2 estimates.

roadmap_text: P0.3 Effort: ~3h for checks 1, 4, 7, 8
objection: 3h for four doctor checks with tests understates scope. Each check needs pre-migration handling, explain-mode remediation strings, and negative tests.
evidence: commands.rs grew past 2000 lines post-P0.3 with check_source_staleness, check_zsh_hook_sourced, check_log_file_sizes, check_watchdog_heartbeat (plus the fail_count aggregator). PR #70 merged 2026-04-24.
suggested_fix: Revise to ~6–8h and call out the 'pre-migration DB' graceful-fallback pattern as a shared sub-task.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED — shipped scope materially larger than 3h; use as calibration for P1.3 (which claims 6h for six new checks).

roadmap_text: P1.1 Scope: hippo watchdog subcommand + capture_alarms table + launchd plist + ack CLI + rate limiter
objection: Violates the guiding principle 'No PR touches more than one logical subsystem.' P1.1 ships: new subcommand, new table+migration, new launchd agent, new user-facing CLI (alarms list/ack), rate-limit/dedup logic, optional osascript path. Six subsystems.
evidence: Roadmap line 8: 'No PR touches more than one logical subsystem.'
suggested_fix: Split into P1.1a (watchdog process + capture_alarms table + rate limiter, gated behind config flag) and P1.1b (ack CLI + launchd install + notify path). Lets reviewers assess policy separately from plumbing.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P1 not started; fix before the P1.1 team launches.

roadmap_text: P2.1 Effort: ~2–3 days
objection: Too aggressive. 06-claude-session-watcher.md commits to: notify-crate FSEvents integration (new dep), partial-line safety, inode/device truncation, 30s per-file timeout + 60s backoff, dual-run parity, unit + integration + end-to-end + property tests, NFS/iCloud detection.
evidence: notify crate not in Cargo.toml today. Property testing with proptest not in the existing test suite. Empirical calibration from P0.2 (A2): review cycles stretch estimates by ~4–6× on complex changes.
suggested_fix: Revise to 4–6 days. Land the watcher behind claude_session_mode='both' in a smaller first PR, then parity tooling + dual-run logging as a second PR.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P2 not started; revise estimate now so the team plans around a realistic window.

roadmap_text: P0.4 Effort: ~2h — OTel source attribute
objection: 2h covers the code diff but misses the Grafana dashboard realignment cost from the rename type→source.
evidence: PR #66 title: 'rename type→source attribute on daemon metrics for source_health alignment' — the rename had ripple effects through dashboards and Prometheus queries.
suggested_fix: Bump estimate to 3–4h in the historical row and note dashboard/rule realignment as a sub-task for future OTel label renames.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED (retroactive) — PR merged 2026-04-24. Use as calibration if another OTel label rename appears in future phases.

roadmap_text: Phase P1 starts after P0.1 AND P0.2 merge
objection: P1 gated on the two longest-pole P0 PRs; the 'parallel agents' framing implied P1 starts Day 2. With realistic P0.2 effort (A2), P1 slips.
evidence: P0 span in reality: 2026-04-23 (P0.1, P0.2, P0.5) through 2026-04-24 (P0.3, P0.4, v0.16.0 release). Roughly 1.5 days wall-clock — not the 0.5 day the roadmap claimed.
suggested_fix: In the roadmap's historical notes, reset the ratio: P0 took 1.5 days with one in-flight fix thread (P0.2). Use 1.5–2 days as the default for similar same-day parallel phases.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: RESOLVED — all of P0 merged; P1 can start. But the historical timing (1.5 days, not 0.5) should be recorded as a planning calibration.

roadmap_text: M4: investigation issues #49–#53 closed with findings
objection: Depends on five specific GitHub issue numbers. Roadmap does not verify they exist or document intended scope.
evidence: PR #57 (the docs PR that introduced the roadmap) body says 'Investigation issues filed as #49-#53.' So they do exist — but the roadmap should still inline a one-liner per issue so reviewers don't need to click through.
suggested_fix: Inline a one-line summary of each issue in 07-roadmap.md or replace with a pointer to a fixed investigation-index doc.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — issues confirmed to exist. Inline summaries still missing.

roadmap_text: P2.2 Verification: Row with probe_tag='synthetic' absent from hippo ask/hippo events; present in source_health
objection: Under-specifies the filter coverage. 05-synthetic-probes.md enumerates ~12 call sites needing WHERE probe_tag IS NULL (daemon ×4, brain server ×2, enrichment, claude_sessions, browser_enrichment, mcp_queries ×4, rag, retrieval).
evidence: grep -rn 'probe_tag' brain/src/ returns 0 — zero brain-side filters exist yet; the full grep list in 05-synthetic-probes.md will be net-new.
suggested_fix: Strengthen verification to (a) a Rust+Python integration test enumerating every event-table SELECT and asserting exclusion, and (b) a Semgrep rule (aligns with .semgrep.yml) flagging new SELECTs that lack probe_tag IS NULL.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P2 not started. The existing .semgrep.yml (shipped via #60) provides a natural home for the new rule.

roadmap_text: P3.1 Blocks: P2.1 dual-run clean 48 h
objection: 'Clean' is undefined. 06-claude-session-watcher.md says 'watcher ≥ tailer, no misses' but doesn't define the measurement mechanism or tolerance.
evidence: 06-claude-session-watcher.md §Migration Plan Phase A exit criterion lacks an assertable predicate.
suggested_fix: Define 'clean' concretely: a scheduled query run hourly that SELECTs per-path row-count deltas between watcher and tailer sources; log divergences to a dedicated table. P3.1 reads that table before flipping default.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P3 not started.

roadmap_text: Consensus review: P0.2 and P0.3 drafted against agreed schema; integrate after P0.1 merges
objection: Understates coupling. P0.3's Check 1 queries source_health, which requires P0.2's write contract (monotonic MAX on last_event_ts, consecutive_failures reset semantics).
evidence: Actual merge order: P0.1 (2026-04-23) → P0.2 (2026-04-23) → P0.3 (2026-04-24). Sequential in practice, not the 'drafted in parallel' framing the roadmap used. P0.3 materially depended on P0.2's semantics being stable.
suggested_fix: For future phases, reword the roadmap to say 'sequential against the write-contract doc (01-source-health.md), consensus review of adjacent PRs before merge.'
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED — sequential was correct. Archive this lesson for P1/P2 phase planning.

roadmap_text: Watchdog: StartInterval=60 in launchd plist; I-7 threshold 180s = 3× interval
objection: launchd StartInterval schedules next run N seconds after previous exit, not wall-clock. Under load, cycles drift and the 3× tolerance shrinks to 2×.
evidence: Apple launchd docs: StartInterval is time since last exit. 04-watchdog.md line 28: StartInterval=60.
suggested_fix: Either switch to StartCalendarInterval for strict wall-clock cadence, or bump I-7 threshold to 5× (300s) to buy headroom under drift. Document the launchd semantics in 04-watchdog.md.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P1.1 not started.

roadmap_text: Check 8 — watchdog heartbeat reads source_health WHERE source='watchdog'
objection: Between P0.3 merge and P1.1 merge, Check 8 has no backing data source. The shipped code falls back gracefully ('no data'), but live installs sit in this state indefinitely until P1.1.
evidence: commands.rs:1299 pre-migration fallback; v0.16.0 release 2026-04-24 is the first shipped version with Check 8 — no watchdog populating 'watchdog' row exists yet.
suggested_fix: Add release-note language for v0.16.0: 'Check 8 returns [--] no data until watchdog ships.' Or gate Check 8 behind a config flag defaulting off until P1.1 lands.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: LIVE — as of v0.16.0, Check 8 is shipped but always returns 'no data.' Currently documented as pre-migration fallback but users may read it as a bug.

roadmap_text: P0.3 covers checks 1, 4, 7, 8; Checks 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10 deferred to P1.3
objection: Check 3 (browser extension dist/ present) is the exact fingerprint of sev1 H2. Deferring it to P1.3 was the wrong call.
evidence: Actually — grep -n 'check_firefox_extension_dist_at' crates/hippo-daemon/src/commands.rs:1498 shows Check 3's functional equivalent already ships (landed in PR #54 sev1 hotfix before the roadmap existed). So Check 3 is in the repo; the roadmap just doesn't reflect that reality.
suggested_fix: Update the roadmap's P1.3 scope to remove Check 3 (already shipped) and optionally note its provenance (#54). Keep remaining checks (2, 5, 6, 9, 10) in P1.3.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — Check 3 exists from #54. Roadmap text is stale.

roadmap_text: P1.2 Scope: Extension heartbeat + NM host forwarding + probe_tag column stub + popup badge
objection: probe_tag column is already in the schema (P0.1, PR #67). Listing a 'stub' in P1.2 is redundant or ambiguous.
evidence: git show 96991b7 --stat shows ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN probe_tag TEXT on three tables. storage.rs:389–391 confirms.
suggested_fix: Remove 'probe_tag column stub' from P1.2 scope. If a probe_tag writer is intended, rename the item to 'probe_tag populate logic' and specify which source.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P1.2 not started; fix the wording before the team launches.

roadmap_text: Overhaul ships in four phases across ~7 days
objection: Math: P0 day 1 + P1 days 2–3 + P2 days 4–7 + 48h dual-run + P3 cleanup → day 9–10, not day 7.
evidence: Roadmap line 3 claims 7 days. P0 actual took 1.5 days already (see A7).
suggested_fix: Restate as '~7 days for feature work, ~10 days to P3 cleanup and tmux-spawn removal.' Adjust based on P0 actuals: if P0 took 1.5 days against 0.5 claimed, assume 3× for similarly-scoped phases.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — update the roadmap headline to reflect actual pace.

roadmap_text: Risk 'Watchdog alarm fatigue during bring-up': High likelihood, Medium impact; mitigation notify_macos=false + conservative thresholds
objection: No rollback plan if alarms still fire too often. Default alarm_rate_limit_minutes=15 is aggressive for a brand-new subsystem.
evidence: 04-watchdog.md §Configuration: alarm_rate_limit_minutes = 15 default.
suggested_fix: Ship P1.1 with alarm_rate_limit_minutes=60 for v0.17, step down to 15 in v0.18 after soak. Or add a 'bring-up mode' flag that suppresses alarms for the first 24 h after watchdog install.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P1.1 not started.

roadmap_text: Risk 'probe_tag filter missing from a query path': Medium/Medium; mitigation = P2.2 integration test
objection: One integration test over ~12 call sites across two languages is fragile. A new query added later has no guard rail.
evidence: 05-synthetic-probes.md §Grep List enumerates 12 files across daemon + brain + MCP.
suggested_fix: Add a Semgrep rule matching SELECT ... FROM (events|claude_sessions|browser_events) without probe_tag IS NULL that fails CI. The .semgrep.yml already exists from #60.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P2 not started.

roadmap_text: 'No big-bang migrations'
objection: P0.1 is schema-level and strictly blocking — every downstream PR depends on it. Calling that 'not big-bang' is a phrasing stretch.
evidence: Roadmap line 16–17: 'P0.1 is the only blocking dependency.'
suggested_fix: Rephrase to 'no big-bang feature migrations; schema-level migrations ship first as an atomic preamble.'
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — one-word doc fix.

roadmap_text: M1: diagnosable via SELECT * FROM source_health in < 5 min without log-diving
objection: No drill, no measurement. '5 min' is a human-timed metric that needs a scripted test.
evidence: 00-overview.md §Success criteria #2 not connected to a test.
suggested_fix: Add an M1 acceptance drill: scripted sev1 simulation (disable Firefox → wait → hippo doctor + SELECT source_health) stopwatched and recorded.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: LIVE — M1 complete (P0 shipped), but drill was not executed. Could be done now against v0.16.0 to ratify M1.

roadmap_text: P0 estimates are per-agent (4h/4h/3h/2h/2h) with 'all five launch simultaneously'
objection: Review cycles not accounted for. Same-day merge of 5 PRs with consensus reviews is aggressive.
evidence: P0 actual wall-clock: 2026-04-23 through 2026-04-24 (1.5 days). Consensus review happened via sequential merge ordering, not same-day.
suggested_fix: Add a 'review + merge' line budget (1h per PR) to phase totals. Use 1.5 days as the revised P0 duration for planning reference.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: VALIDATED — P0 took 3× the claimed 0.5 days.

roadmap_text: Risk register omits: daemon.rs touched by multiple P0 PRs
objection: P0.2 modifies flush_events in daemon.rs; P0.4 also touched metric call sites inside flush paths (the type→source rename).
evidence: P0.2 merged 2026-04-23 (PR #68); P0.4 merged 2026-04-24 (PR #66). Sequential merge, one rebase cycle — no actual conflict, but the risk existed.
suggested_fix: For future phases with overlapping hot files, call out the merge order explicitly in the roadmap ('merge P0.4 first, then P0.2 rebased').
status_as_of_v0_16_0: RESOLVED — sequential merge avoided the conflict. Keep as planning-heuristic for P1/P2.

roadmap_text: P1.3 Effort: ~6h for doctor checks 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10
objection: 6h for 6 checks is 1h per check. Check 5 (live-JSONL vs DB) + Check 6 (tail + awk + SQL) + Check 2 (NM manifest parse) are individually non-trivial. P0.3 calibration (A3) supports the objection.
evidence: 03-doctor-upgrades.md Check 5 requires glob + per-file readline + SQL. Check 6 parses session-hook-debug.log. Check 2 parses NM manifest JSON and stats referenced binary.
suggested_fix: Revise to 1–1.5 days. Additionally: drop Check 3 from P1.3 (already shipped via #54) so the remaining scope is 5 checks.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P1.3 not started.

roadmap_text: P3.2: Delete tmux-spawn from shell/claude-session-hook.sh; reduce to marker write
objection: Deleting tmux-spawn removes the backstop that kept sessions ingesting when the watcher was down between P2.1 and P3.1.
evidence: 08-anti-patterns.md AP-9 is strict about removing fallbacks. Tmux-spawn is not JSONL fallback but is a recovery path.
suggested_fix: Before P3.2 merges, require 7-day clean window (not 48h) and add revert instructions to the hook header. Preserve hippo ingest claude-session --batch as the documented manual recovery.
status_as_of_v0_16_0: OPEN — P3 not started.
